violations.description: Time as a public health control. — --Food was not marked or identified with the time within which it must be cooked, served, or discarded. -Wrapped hamburgers do not have time stamps or log to monitor hold times.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -Cases of Frito Lay chips on floor in dry storage. -Single use cups/lids on floor and/or next to snow shovels at storage across from dry storage room.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. -Nacho dispenser found at 114F (bag had been in warmer since previous day service). *Minimal product left, was disposed of while on-site. Directed staff to dispose of newely opened bag at 5:15pm and to monitor and/or have dispenser fixed.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Dried food debris at interior surfaces of Tostitos cheese dispenser. -Frost build up at upper corner edges of freezers.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Food trays and paper wraps are not stored inverted to protect food contact surfaces (at pizza hot hold).
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
